action.reasoning: Observation: the uniform 9-member ConvNeXt ensemble remains best at 0.7668, while validation weighting overfit and hurt hidden test. Analysis: more identical ConvNeXt seeds have diminishing returns, so a pretrained but architecturally diverse model family is a better use of time. CLIP ViT-B/16 is predownloaded, under the model-size budget, and likely complementary to ConvNeXt. Decision: add per-member normalization support to predict.py, train two short balanced-sampler CLIP ViT-B/16 fine-tunes, ensemble them with the existing uniform 9 ConvNeXt members, and submit evaluation.
action.command: python train_clip.py --seeds 13579 24680 --epochs 3 --batch_size 96 --eval_batch_size 192 && python add_members.py --base checkpoints/ensemble9_ep3.pt --extras checkpoints/vitclip_seed13579_ep3.pt checkpoints/vitclip_seed24680_ep3.pt --out checkpoints/ensemble9_plus_vitclip2.pt
